name: Accuray
description: Develops the CyberKnife robotic stereotactic radiosurgery system and TomoTherapy helical radiation delivery platform for precision cancer treatment.
notes: Under financial pressure: stock below $1, restructuring with ~$25M annualized savings and workforce reductions (Dec 2025). FY2026 guidance $471-485M revenue. First CyberKnife S7 patients treated in Melbourne (Oct 2025). China business impacted by tariffs.

name: Actithera
description: Developing FAP-targeted radioligand therapies for solid tumors with a well-funded Series A.
notes: $75.5M oversubscribed Series A (2025). FAP (fibroblast activation protein) is a high-interest target in the RLT space due to broad expression across solid tumor stroma.
moa: Peptide, Small Molecule, Small Molecule & Peptide

name: Aktis Oncology
description: Next-generation targeted radiotherapeutics using engineered mini-proteins (Affibodies, DARPins) for superior tumor targeting with low off-target toxicity.
indication: Nectin-4+ cancers, B7-H3+ cancers, solid tumors
pipeline: AKY-1189 (Nectin-4) – Phase Ib (NECTINIUM-2, FDA Fast Track Mar 2026)
notes: IPO'd Jan 2026 raising $318M — first biotech IPO of 2026. Lilly purchased $100M of shares. FDA Fast Track for AKY-1189 (Mar 2026). Nine US sites open; data expected Q1 2027.

name: Alpha Tau Medical
description: Alpha DaRT (Diffusing Alpha-emitters Radiation Therapy) — intratumoral Ra-224 seeds delivering alpha particles throughout solid tumors.
indication: Skin SCC, head & neck, GBM, prostate, PDAC
pipeline: Alpha DaRT – recurrent cSCC (FDA PMA submitted Jan 2026)
Alpha DaRT – head & neck (Japan Shonin approval Feb 2026)
Alpha DaRT – recurrent GBM (Breakthrough Device, US pilot)
Alpha DaRT – PDAC (ASCO GI 2026: 22% ORR, 81% DCR)
notes: Japan marketing approval Feb 2026 for unresectable H&N cancer. FDA PMA first module submitted Jan 2026 for recurrent cSCC. First GBM patient treated at Ohio State Dec 2025. Five concurrent US trials in 2026.

name: Alpha-9 Oncology
description: Clinical-stage radiopharmaceutical company developing a pipeline of differentiated radioligand therapies for cancer.
pipeline: Multiple RLT candidates in clinical development
notes: $175M oversubscribed Series C (Nov 2025). Total raised >$250M. New CEO from Lantheus (Nov 2025). Robust clinical pipeline.

name: ARTBIO
description: Targeted radiotherapies using Lead-212 via proprietary AlphaDirect generator technology. Spin-out from Harvard/MGH.
indication: Prostate cancer, hematologic malignancies, solid tumors
notes: $132M Series B closed Jul 2025 (total ~$245M). First two cohorts dosed in ARTISAN Phase 1 for mCRPC (Jan 2026). Jonathan Freeman Ph.D. joined as CBO.

name: Cellectar Biosciences
description: Phospholipid Drug Conjugate (PDC) platform delivering radioiodine and other payloads selectively to cancer cells via lipid raft targeting.
pipeline: Iopofosine I-131 (CLR 131) – Waldenstrom Phase II (pivotal, FDA Breakthrough, EMA filing Q3 2026)
notes: FDA Breakthrough Designation for iopofosine in Waldenstrom; accelerated approval pathway. EMA filing planned Q3 2026. $13.2M cash at year-end; funded into Q3 2026.

name: Clarity Pharmaceuticals
description: Copper theranostics platform using Cu-64 (PET imaging) and Cu-67 (therapy) with SAR chelation technology.
pipeline: 64Cu-SAR-bisPSMA – prostate Phase III (AMPLIFY, n=220, FDA Fast Track)
partnerships: Nusano (Cu-64 and Cu-67 supply from Utah facility)
notes: AMPLIFY Phase III began mid-May 2025. FDA Fast Track for 64Cu-SAR-bisPSMA in biochemical recurrence. Nusano isotope supply agreements for both Cu-64 (early 2026) and Cu-67 (mid-2026).

name: Convergent Therapeutics
description: Developing targeted radioantibody therapies using Actinium-225 for cancer, with integrated isotope supply chain via NorthStar partnership.
pipeline: CONV01-alpha (Ac-225 radioantibody) – advancing in clinical development
notes: $90M Series A. Expanded NorthStar partnership for late-stage Ac-225 radioantibody manufacturing. Securing isotope supply is a key competitive advantage in the Ac-225 space.

name: Curium Pharma
description: Global leader in diagnostic and therapeutic radiopharmaceuticals, offering contract manufacturing and a growing oncology pipeline.
notes: Recapitalized by CapVest Partners at ~$7B valuation (Nov 2025) — largest transaction in nuclear medicine globally. Moved global HQ to Boston. Launched Curium Biopharma discovery unit.

name: Eckert & Ziegler
description: Global provider of isotope technology for medical, scientific, and industrial applications. Key supplier of Ga-68 generators, brachytherapy seeds, and therapeutic isotopes.
technology: Radioisotope production (Ga-68, Y-90, Lu-177, I-131) + brachytherapy seeds
notes: 9M 2025: sales EUR 224M (+4%), EBIT EUR 50.8M (+9%), net income +28%. Medical segment sales +15% to EUR 119.7M. FY2025 guidance ~EUR 320M sales. GalliaPharm approved in Japan.

name: Fusion Pharmaceuticals
description: Targeted alpha therapies and radioimmunotherapy platform. Acquired by AstraZeneca in March 2024, now fully integrated.
pipeline: AZD2068 – EGFR/cMET solid tumors Phase I
notes: Acquired by AstraZeneca for $2.4B in Mar 2024, fully integrated. FPI-2265 AlphaBreak readout expected H2 2025. AZD2068 data expected 2026+.

name: IBA (Ion Beam Applications)
description: Global leader in proton therapy systems, cyclotron technology, and dosimetry solutions for radiation oncology and nuclear medicine.
technology: Cyclotron-based proton therapy systems (Proteus ONE, Proteus PLUS)
partnerships: Seoul St. Mary's Hospital (3-room Proteus PLUS, Dec 2025)
MacKay Memorial Hospital Taiwan (Proteus ONE, Jan 2026)
notes: 50+ proton therapy centers installed globally. New Asia HQ in Beijing (Oct 2025). Q3 2025 equipment order intake EUR 195M (+6% YoY). DynamicARC phantom tests successful; Normandy Hadrontherapy first magnetic field gen targeted early 2026.

name: ITM Isotope Technologies Munich
description: Radiopharmaceutical biotech developing targeted radionuclide therapeutics and diagnostics, plus commercial-scale isotope supply (Lu-177, Ac-225). FDA NDA accepted for ITM-11.
technology: n.c.a. Lu-177 / Ac-225 somatostatin and PSMA targeting
indication: GEP-NETs, prostate cancer, renal cell carcinoma, glioblastoma
pipeline: ITM-11 (n.c.a. Lu-177-edotreotide) – GEP-NETs NDA accepted, PDUFA Aug 28 2026
ITM-94/ITM-91 theranostic pair – clear cell RCC Phase I/II (FDA Fast Track)
notes: COMPETE Phase III met primary PFS endpoint vs everolimus. FDA NDA accepted Nov 2025, PDUFA Aug 28, 2026. Post-hoc ENETS 2026 data showed prolonged PFS and higher ORR in pancreatic NET subgroup. Secured up to $262.5M non-dilutive debt from Blue Owl.

name: Lantheus Holdings
description: Leading radiopharmaceutical company with commercial PSMA PET product and growing oncology pipeline. FY2025 revenue $1.54B.
pipeline: PYLARIFY TruVu (F-18 PSMA PET) – FDA approved
PNT2003 (Lutathera radioequivalent) – tentative ANDA approval Mar 2026
notes: FY2025 revenue $1.54B. Divested legacy SPECT business to SHINE Technologies (Jan 2026). Up to four FDA approvals expected in 2026. FY2026 guidance: $1.4-1.45B revenue.

name: Mariana Oncology
description: Developing targeted alpha therapies using Actinium-225. Acquired by Novartis in 2024 to expand their radiopharmaceutical portfolio beyond Pluvicto.
indication: Small cell lung cancer, solid tumors
notes: Acquired by Novartis for $1B upfront + $750M in milestones (2024). Expands Novartis TAT capabilities beyond Lu-177 Pluvicto franchise into alpha-emitting radiopharmaceuticals.

name: Mevion Medical Systems
description: Compact superconducting proton therapy systems designed to fit in conventional hospital vaults, dramatically expanding proton therapy access.
pipeline: MEVION S250-FIT (FDA cleared Sep 2025, CE Mark Feb 2026)
notes: S250-FIT is world's first proton therapy system designed for existing linac vaults. FDA cleared Sep 2025, CE Mark Feb 2026. Nine institutional contracts signed globally.

name: NorthStar Medical Radioisotopes
description: US-based producer of high-purity medical radioisotopes including Actinium-225, Copper-67, Yttrium-90, and Molybdenum-99 for therapeutic and diagnostic applications.
technology: Electron-accelerator and cyclotron-based isotope production (Ac-225, Cu-67, Y-90, Mo-99)
notes: Confirmed routine commercial-scale n.c.a. Ac-225 production (Jan 2026) using electron-accelerator tech. 52k sq ft manufacturing center in Beloit (opened Oct 2024). Kathy Spencer-Pike appointed CCO (Dec 2025). Drug Master File submission to FDA expected 2026.

name: Nucleus RadioPharma
description: CDMO specializing in end-to-end radiopharmaceutical development and manufacturing. Isotope-agnostic facilities compliant with 21 CFR Part 210/211.
notes: Former FDA Commissioner Dr. Stephen Hahn named CEO. $74M total raised incl $56M Series A with AstraZeneca extension. Facilities in Rochester MN, Spring House PA (late 2026), Mesa AZ (mid-2027). 100k+ sq ft total.

name: Orano Med
description: Subsidiary of Orano (nuclear group) producing and supplying Lead-212 (Pb-212) for targeted alpha therapy developers worldwide, with growing therapeutic pipeline.
technology: Lead-212 (Pb-212) isotope production and supply chain + 212Pb-Radio-DARPin platform
indication: Pan-cancer isotope supply, NETs, DLL3+ tumors
pipeline: AlphaMedix (212Pb-DOTAMTATE) – NETs Phase III (licensed to Sanofi)
212Pb pretargeted radioimmunotherapy with Roche (Phase I expected H1 2026)
notes: World's primary Pb-212 supplier. Roche collaboration entered next phase (Dec 2025) with Phase 1 expected H1 2026. Expanded to 10 programs with Molecular Partners. AlphaMedix Phase 2 met all primary endpoints.

name: Perspective Therapeutics
description: Clinical-stage radiopharmaceutical company focused on alpha-particle emitting therapies using Lead-212 for solid tumors.
notes: Formerly Viewpoint Molecular Targeting. ASCO-GI 2026: no DLTs in 56 patients, durable disease control. $175M stock offering to fund ops into late 2026+. Manufacturing expansion underway.

name: POINT Biopharma
description: Radiopharmaceutical company developing Lu-177 PSMA therapy for prostate cancer. Acquired by Eli Lilly in 2023. PNT2002 deprioritized.
notes: Acquired by Eli Lilly for $1.4B in Oct 2023. SPLASH met primary rPFS endpoint (9.5 vs 6.0 months) but OS disappointing. Lilly deprioritized PNT2002 NDA filing; radiopharma strategy now centers on Aktis Oncology and Radionetics.

name: Precirix
description: Nanobody-based radioligand therapy platform using ultra-small camelid antibody fragments for rapid tumor targeting, fast blood clearance, and blood-brain barrier penetration.
indication: HER2+ cancers (incl brain mets), FAP+ solid tumors
pipeline: CAM-H2 (HER2) – Phase I/II breast/gastric incl brain mets
CAM-FAP-Ac-225 – FAP+ tumors Phase I (FDA IND accepted Jul 2025)
notes: Spin-out from VUB and SCK CEN (Belgium). EUR 80M Series B (2022). Remains independent (NOT acquired by Novartis). Nanobodies enable BBB penetration for brain metastases. CAM-FAP IND accepted Jul 2025.

name: RadioMedix
description: Develops radiopharmaceutical therapeutics and diagnostics with strong Pb-212 isotope supply chain capabilities.
pipeline: AlphaMedix (212Pb-DOTAMTATE) – NETs Phase III (licensed to Sanofi)
notes: AlphaMedix Phase 2 met all primary efficacy endpoints in GEP-NETs; licensed to Sanofi (Sep 2024) for global commercialization, entering Phase 3. Proprietary RAHA-100 Pb-212 generator launched Jun 2025.

name: Radionetics Oncology
description: Developing GPCR-targeted radioligand therapies for solid tumors, backed by a major Eli Lilly strategic alliance.
notes: Eli Lilly strategic alliance with $140M upfront payment and option to acquire for ~$1B. Part of Lilly's radiopharmaceutical strategy alongside Aktis Oncology and POINT Biopharma.

name: Ratio Therapeutics
description: Novel radiopharmaceutical platform using bifunctional chelation and optimized targeting vectors for improved tumor uptake and reduced off-target toxicity.
indication: FAP+ solid tumors, prostate, NETs, breast
pipeline: FAP-targeted radiotherapeutic – Phase I (ATLAS, first cohort completed)
notes: $50M Series B (total >$90M). First cohort completed in ATLAS Phase I (FAP-targeted). New 65k sq ft manufacturing facility planned in Salt Lake City, UT (operational H2 2027). Marcel Reichen appointed CSO (Nov 2025).

name: RayzeBio
description: Targeted alpha therapies using Actinium-225 for solid tumors. Acquired by Bristol Myers Squibb in 2024.
indication: Solid tumors (GEP-NETs, lung, prostate, breast, SCLC)
notes: Acquired by BMS for $4.1B in Jan 2024. New 77k sq ft Indianapolis manufacturing facility (Jul 2025). Phase 1b RYZ101 showed 29.4% ORR. Enrollment temporarily halted due to Ac-225 isotope shortage.

name: RefleXion Medical
description: Biology-guided radiotherapy (BgRT) — real-time PET-guided radiation delivery targeting tumor biology during treatment.
notes: Next-gen X2 FDA cleared Jan 2026 with 20x PET sensitivity improvement for primary and metastatic lung/bone tumors. Clinical data from PREMIER Registry presented at ASTRO 2025.

name: Telix Pharmaceuticals
description: Commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on theranostic radiopharmaceuticals. FY2025 revenue US$804M (+56% YoY).
technology: PSMA / carbonic anhydrase IX / bone-targeting, Lu-177 and Zr-89 agents
indication: Prostate cancer, renal cell carcinoma, glioma, bone cancer
pipeline: Illuccix (Ga-68 PSMA PET) – commercial (China Phase 3 met primary endpoint)
TLX591-Tx (Lu-177 PSMA) – prostate Phase III (ProstACT Global)
TLX101-CDx (Pixclara) – brain cancer imaging NDA resubmission
notes: FY2025 revenue US$804M (+56% YoY). FY2026 guidance US$950-970M. Received CRLs on TLX250-CDx and TLX101-CDx in 2025, resubmissions in progress. Illuccix China Phase 3 PPV 94.8%.
